parsed_review.summary: CP00-750 is a descriptor-only RP24 Korean Legal Depth evidence-workflow bridge pack (range RP24.P08.M03.S01-RP24.P08.M04.S18, risk B, 40 units). Independent read-only inspection confirms the implementation is sound and internally consistent: registry.js defines KOREAN_LEGAL_CP750_PACK_BINDING (pack_id CP00-750, risk B, 40 units, upstream CP00-749, next CP00-751, next_subphase RP24.P08.M04.S19, gates H24/C24) and KOREAN_LEGAL_CP750_REQUIREMENTS whose deliverable_counts (hermes_evidence 20, claude_review 2, implementation 17, test 1) and micro rows (M03=22, M04=18 via EVIDENCE_WORKFLOW_ROWS = CP749 20 rows + Documentation update + Operator summary) exactly match the pack baseline/prompt distributions. service.js wires the case set, descriptor, Hermes evidence packet, Claude review packet, and closeout handoff, chaining from CP749 with authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-751, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false). validators.js performs meaningful coverage and no-leak/descriptor-only/no-runtime checks; index.js re-exports all required symbols; test/model.test.js adds three CP750 tests (binding/coverage, descriptor/packets runtime-closed, contract-projection-latest) asserting zero errors; contracts/korean-legal-depth-contract.json is regenerated with latest_pack CP00-750, validation.valid=true, and CP722-749 preserved as historical; README.md adds an accurate CP00-750 section. command-evidence.json records all 23 commands (node --check on all sources, node --test, rp24/closeout/plan/spec/weighted/fullplan/goal/control-plane validates, npm test, npm build, git diff --check) at exit_code 0, with no_real_data=true and writes_product_state=false. Boundaries are intact: no real client/matter/legal data, no product-state writes, no HWPX/court-filing/litigation/corporate-document/clause runtime opened, Hermes used only for descriptor-only evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false), and Claude remains a read-only non-final reviewer. The pack transparently reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status in_progress/pending_review across manifest, construction-inspection, claude-review-result, and adjudication — it does not overclaim runtime or enterprise trust, so production_ready gates remain un-weakened. CP178+ review hardening preconditions hold: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1449-byte valid-JSON stdout), tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob with no forbidden tools, prompt 2451 bytes (< 25600) with no embedded full diff/source, and review-schema present. The pending blocks_pack_closeout/blocks_goal_closeout flags in the deliverables are the default pre-review gate that this PASS resolves; per the stated authority boundary, goal closeout is not blocked merely because Claude is not the final human/enterprise-trust approver. No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues found.
